data.title: TotalEnergies CEO to speak at Barclays conference on Sept 4 at 10:20am EDT. Global company in oil and biofuel.
data.description: The Chairman & CEO of TotalEnergies, listed on Paris, London, and New York stock exchanges as TTE, will participate in a virtual fireside chat at the Barclays 39th Annual CEO Energy-Power Conference on September 4th at 10:20am EDT. To watch the live webcast, please click on the provided link. A replay will also be accessible later in the day. TotalEnergies is a leading global energy company involved in the production and distribution of oil and biofuels.

data.title: Kevin O'Leary notes Gen Z's investment in Bitcoin, Ethereum & stocks; admires their ease of access - not seen during his early investment journey.
data.description: Renowned investor Kevin O'Leary shared insights on the investment habits of Gen Z in a recent interview, noting their growing interest in cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin (BTC) and Ethereum (ETH) in addition to traditional stocks. O'Leary, also known as "Mr. Wonderful," mentioned how younger investors are now utilizing platforms such as Robinhood to purchase these assets alongside stocks, a shift from the norms prevalent when he began investing. He emphasized that Gen Z considers cryptocurrencies as viable investment options akin to stocks or bonds, demonstrating a marked departure from traditional investment strategies. This trend highlights a significant evolution in the investment landscape among younger generations.

data.title: Alibaba expected to report earnings at $1.95/share on $34.26B revenue. Autodesk raises full-year guidance.
data.description: US stock futures are down this morning, and investors are eyeing key stocks. Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. (NYSE: BABA) is expected to announce earnings of $1.95 per share on revenue of $34.26 billion. After hours, Alibaba shares climbed 0.5% to $120.20. Autodesk Inc. (NASDAQ: ADSK) surpassed expectations in its second-quarter financial results. The company raised its full-year guidance from $6.92-$7 billion to $7.03-$7.08 billion, against a $6.97 billion estimate. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Gevo's RNG subsidiary refinances $40 million debt with new bond sales, strengthening the company's balance sheet. Balancesheet liquidity increased by $30 million.
data.description: Gevo, Inc. (NASDAQ: GEVO) based in Englewood, Colorado, recently announced the purchase of $40 million non-recourse tax-exempt private activity bonds by Barclays Capital Inc. The bonds, known as the “2025 Bonds,” were issued by the Iowa Finance Authority on behalf of Gevo’s subsidiary, Gevo NW Iowa RNG, LLC (“Gevo RNG”). This transaction resulted in the refinancing of $40 million from the previously issued Iowa Finance Authority Solid Waste Facility Revenue Bonds (Gevo NW Iowa RNG, LLC Renewable Natural Gas Project), Series 2021 (Green Bonds), valued at $68.2 million. These bonds were backed by an irrevocable direct pay letter of credit. By refinancing, Gevo was able to unlock $40 million of restricted cash securing the letter of credit, boosting its balance sheet liquidity by around $30 million following expenses related to the 2025 Bonds.
